approach: Differentiate the three displayed coordinate polynomials symbolically, factor the determinant, then evaluate the map at the three announced rational points using exact arithmetic.
summary: Independent reproduction returned determinant -2 and the same image (-1/4, 0, 0) for all three distinct inputs.
rationale: This checks the two decisive facts directly and makes no appeal to model judgment.
claims: The displayed map has constant Jacobian determinant -2.
The three displayed rational inputs are distinct and map to (-1/4, 0, 0).
next_steps: Package the script and dependency lock as the calibration certificate.
tool_disclosure: SymPy exact arithmetic; verification run by Codex for Charlie Krug.

approach: Exact divisor-incidence sieve with the recurrence R(n+1)=max(R(n), n+tau(n)), independently audited by trial-division factorization and direct maximization.
summary: No candidate was found. The C search exactly checked every n in [25, 10^7], with minimum R(n)-n equal to 3 at n=35. A separate Python trial-division audit agreed through 200,000 and recovered the known terms 5, 8, 10, 12, 24. This does not improve the stronger reported human computational bounds.
rationale: The sieve counts tau(x) by its defining divisor incidences, and the record recurrence is an exact identity. The audit uses a distinct tau implementation and direct maximum. Both checks are bounded only.
claims: The executed C program excludes every n with 25 <= n <= 10^7: max_{m<n}(m+tau(m)) >= n+3.
The independently audited range 25 <= n <= 200000 has no candidate.
evidence: UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer run passed through 10^6; AddressSanitizer's LeakSanitizer could not run under the traced environment.
next_steps: Independently review or run the reported frontier certificate through approximately 6.16e17.
Formalize the record recurrence and the finite sieve certificate, then investigate the reported prime-chain reduction rather than extending an already dominated raw scan.
tool_disclosure: Used web search/opening for source and literature checks; wrote C and Python code; compiled with cc; ran Python 3 and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer. No CAS, proof assistant, SMT/SAT solver, external data set, or sub-agents used.

approach: Exact classification of the primes p=24m+1 lacking Xu's restricted 'tame' solutions, followed by factor-pair certificates for the residual wild primes.
summary: Derived an exhaustive tame criterion p=4abt-a-b with gcd(a,b)=1, reproduced Xu's two published checkpoints, and extended the tame/wild classification from m<=30000 to m<=1000000. Among 187948 relevant primes p<=24000001, exactly 14 were wild: 409, 577, 5569, 9601, 23929, 83449, 102001, 329617, 712321, 1134241, 1724209, 1726201, 5212561, 8813281. All 14 have exact strict Erdős-Straus decompositions with first-denominator offset at most 5. This does not improve the known global verification through 10^18, and the larger tame/wild classification still needs a fully independent whole-range implementation.
rationale: The enumeration uses only forced finite ranges, deterministic sieving, divisor enumeration, and integer equalities. It exactly reproduces Xu's counts of 591 primes/5 wild for m<=2000 and 7185 primes/9 wild for m<=30000. A slower definition-level implementation agrees for all 325 relevant primes through 25000, and every stored decomposition was checked by cross multiplication. The factor-pair lemma and low-offset method already occur in recent literature, so only the extended tame/wild cutoff is treated as progress; novelty or priority is not asserted.
claims: For a prime p=24m+1, Xu-tame solvability is equivalent to the existence of coprime positive integers a<=b and t with p=4abt-a-b; it is enough to enumerate 4a^2-2a<=p and divisors b of p+a.
Exact computation found precisely 14 Xu-wild primes p=24m+1 for 0<=m<=1000000, from 187948 primes examined.
Each of those 14 primes has a strict decomposition x<y<z with x=ceil(p/4)+h and 0<=h<=5.
For every n congruent to 129 or 333 modulo 476, n>=129, the formulas x=(n+7)/4, y=(nx+17)/7, z=nx(nx+17)/(7*17) give a strict Erdős-Straus decomposition; this family is not claimed new.
evidence: research_note.md: proofs, literature comparison, complete wild list, limitations, and reproducibility ledger.
search_wild.py: deterministic exact tame enumeration and unrestricted factor-pair witness generator.
Command: python3 search_wild.py --max-m 1000000 > wild_m1000000.json; scope p=24m+1, m<=1000000; output 187948 primes and 14 wild.
Command: python3 search_wild.py --max-m 1000000 --unrestricted --max-offset 1000 > wild_m1000000_witnesses.json; exact strict witnesses for all 14.
Command: python3 verify_results.py; checked slow/fast tame equivalence for 325 primes p<=25000, all 14 stored identities, and 200 instances of the residue family.
next_steps: Write an independently implemented streaming certificate and validator for nonexistence of tame parameters for all 14 wild rows across the full p<=24000001 enumeration.
Extend the classification using a segmented sieve so the bound can grow without a full smallest-prime-factor table.
Test whether repeated factor pairs (q,d)=(3,5), (7,17), and (7,52) impose a provable structural condition on future Xu-wild primes.
Seek independent human review of the tame-equivalence proof and a more exhaustive literature check before asserting novelty.
tool_disclosure: A single GPT-5 Codex attempt performed the reasoning and wrote the artifacts. Internet search/open tools were used for source inspection. Python 3 standard-library code performed deterministic sieving, factorization, divisor enumeration, JSON generation, and exact integer checks; shell commands ran tests and SHA-256. No CAS, SMT/SAT solver, probabilistic numerical package, or proof assistant was run. The third-party Lean formalization was inspected but not executed or modified.

approach: Independent streaming certificate and whole-range validation of Xu-tame primes p=24m+1 for 0<=m<=1000000.

strategy.mechanism: Emit one exact (a,b,t) witness for every tame prime and complete factorizations of p+a across the forced a-range for every wild prime; validate coverage and all algebra with a separate implementation.

hypothesis: Exactly 14 primes p=24m+1 with 0<=m<=1000000 fail p=4abt-a-b for coprime integers 1<=a<=b and t>=1.
discriminating_test: A separate validator must reconstruct every progression prime, verify all tame identities, exhaust every divisor b|p+a for every wild row, and reproduce the claimed counts and list.
summary: The independent generator produced 187948 prime records: 187934 tame and 14 wild. The independent validator accepted the full 10,231,469-byte certificate, reproduced Xu's 591/5 and 7185/9 checkpoints, and confirmed the wild list 409, 577, 5569, 9601, 23929, 83449, 102001, 329617, 712321, 1134241, 1724209, 1726201, 5212561, 8813281. This does not improve the known global verification through 10^18.
rationale: Positive cases carry directly checked integer witnesses. Negative cases carry 5667 complete prime-factorization rows covering every mathematically possible a for the 14 wild primes; the validator independently regenerates every possible divisor b. Prime coverage is reconstructed separately, and three rehashed semantic corruptions were rejected for their intended mathematical reasons.
claims: Among the 187948 primes p=24m+1 with 0<=m<=1000000, exactly 14 are Xu-wild.
The certificate supplies strict Erdős–Straus decompositions induced by tame parameters for every one of the other 187934 primes.
Novelty is only a candidate: searches found no longer published tame/wild classification, but priority and peer review remain unresolved.
evidence: Validator definition-level search agreed with the certificate for all 325 relevant primes p<=25000.
next_steps: Give only Xu's theorem, the criterion, certificate, and file hash to an isolated reviewer.
Require a checker in Rust, C, or Lean that does not inspect or reuse either Python implementation.
If it agrees, prepare a short computational note and ask Xu whether the extended classification is already known.

approach: Fail-closed authentication and independent validation of the R(5,5) order-42 control corpus and provisional two-conflict K43 seed

strategy.mechanism: Freeze exact source-byte discriminators, require them before computation, compare independent Python/direct-combination and C/recursive-bitset evaluators, and cross-check graph6 plumbing with nauty and NetworkX

hypothesis: The authoritative McKay corpus is locally available with the frozen 47,888-byte SHA-256 discriminator and can then reproduce 656 valid, canonically distinct controls under two independent exact checkers.
discriminating_test: Require the local corpus to have SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb, size 47,888, and 328 records before running any graph check; otherwise record source_preflight_failed with downstream_checks_run=false.
summary: The exact corpus bytes could not be acquired in the managed environment. The source preflight therefore failed closed and no corpus graph was checked. Separately, the provisional K43 matrix control passed both exact checkers and all mutation/rejection tests, and graph6 plumbing passed an exhaustive 1,044-class order-7 adversarial suite against nauty and NetworkX. No Ramsey bound, witness, classification, or exclusion is claimed.
rationale: The epoch produced independently replayable control infrastructure and eliminated parser/complement behavior as an immediate ambiguity, while respecting the source-authentication stop condition. Missing authoritative bytes prevent promotion beyond internal progress.
claims: For the exact unauthenticated transcription with SHA-256 3beebde4f3120337029bb24de1f737f72eab567cbe92c77f79e7206ce4c304e9, two independent checkers agree that graph2 has exactly the K5s {0,15,22,28,39} and {0,15,24,28,39}; deleting 0, 15, 28, or 39 yields a valid order-42 graph.

Across one representative of every 1,044 unlabeled simple graph class on seven vertices, the two graph6 checkers agree exactly, NetworkX agrees on graph signatures, and the complement encoding agrees byte-for-byte with nauty-complg.
The authoritative order-42 corpus was absent locally, so zero corpus or complement instances were checked this epoch.
evidence: CHECKPOINT.md contains exact replay command, hashes, scope limits, and stop conditions
next_steps: Acquire unchanged McKay bytes at sources/r55_42some.g6 while recording timestamp, headers, final URL, byte count, and hash.
If and only if the frozen byte discriminator matches, run the exact authenticated-corpus experiment command recorded in CHECKPOINT.md.
After that gate passes, acquire and authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4, then canonicalize the four valid deletion controls against the supplied 656.
Do not begin unknown-42 or K43 repair search until both raw-source gates pass.

Fail-closed source authentication -> missing or altered bytes must prevent downstream mathematical claims -> the absent corpus produced an explicit preflight failure with no graph checks.

approach: Mandatory source/literature baseline followed by a scoped Lean interface draft and bounded independent semantic controls

strategy.mechanism: Translate the maintained min-max statement using Finset, Nat.findGreatest, and Mathlib asymptotic equivalence; test the Sidon semantics independently before repository-pinned elaboration.

hypothesis: The proposed four-variable Sidon predicate represents uniqueness of unordered pair sums with repetition, and the resulting bounded extremal interface can express the explicit conjecture ell(N) ~ sqrt(N).
discriminating_test: Compare the four-variable predicate with an independently implemented unordered-pair-sum encoding on every subset of {-4,...,4}, then attempt Lean elaboration far enough to identify whether the local environment contains the required repository dependencies.
summary: The mandatory baseline was completed. A May 2026 preprint postdating the official page's last edit was identified and incorporated. A Lean candidate defines IsSidon, GuaranteedSidonSize, ell via Nat.findGreatest, maximality API lemmas, three controls, and the answer(sorry)-scoped asymptotic declaration. Two independent semantic encodings agreed on all 512 subsets of {-4,...,4}; all seven adversarial controls passed. The Lean candidate was not elaborated because this environment lacks FormalConjectures and Mathlib. Two low-memory diagnostics also failed while creating a Lean thread; a controlled 4096 MB single-thread run reached the actual unknown-module error.
rationale: The source and current literature audit identifies a legitimate unfilled formalization target, and the bounded checker rejects the two most likely semantic mistranslations. However, finite semantic agreement is not kernel validation, and no repository build, CI, or human statement review has occurred. Progress rather than candidate status is therefore warranted.
claims: As accessed on 2026-07-20, Erdős Problem #530 is marked open, asks whether ell(N) ~ sqrt(N), and reports no formalised statement.

Bailleul and Riblet's arXiv:2605.03181v1 reports the lower bound (1/(3 sqrt 3)+o(1)) sqrt(n) for arbitrary finite subsets of the integers and reals, without reaching the conjectured leading constant 1.
The literal four-variable Sidon predicate and unordered-pair uniqueness agree on every subset of {-4,...,4}; this is a finite computed fact only.
FormalConjectures/ErdosProblems/530.lean is a drafted, unelaborated candidate and is not yet a completed formalization.
evidence: .proof-experiments/20260720-194758-ee6344/stdout.txt: 512 subsets, 140 Sidon subsets, zero mismatches, seven controls true
next_steps: In a network-enabled checkout of google-deepmind/formal-conjectures, copy the candidate file and run lake build FormalConjectures.ErdosProblems.530 at the repository-pinned Lean 4.27.0 and Mathlib commit.
If the target build passes, run the full lake build and inspect that sorry occurs only in the answer placeholder and open conjecture proof.
Request assignment on issue #773 and obtain human review of repeated summands, swapped-pair triviality, exact-size guarantees, Nat.findGreatest maximality, and the ~[atTop]/Real.sqrt translation.
Resolve the literature-note discrepancy between Abbott's abstract constant c < 2/25 and the 2026 preprint's description of the prior constant as 0.0805.

independent_checker: checks/check_sidon_semantics.py uses unordered pairs with repetition, materially different from the candidate's four-variable implication. It exhaustively compared both encodings on all subsets of {-4,...,4}. It does not elaborate Lean or validate the asymptotic statement.
transfer_insights: Mathlib's Ruzsa-Szemeredi extremal-number definition uses Nat.findGreatest -> prediction: the same bounded idiom gives a short maximality API for ell -> drafted successfully but repository elaboration remains pending.
Weak-Sidon literature excludes repeated summands -> prediction: that encoding will miss 0+2=1+1 in {0,1,2} -> observed, so the transfer was rejected for Problem #530.

strategy_proposals.hypothesis: An independent reviewer will recover the same unordered-pair, exact-cardinality, and asymptotic semantics.
strategy_proposals.discriminating_test: Record the first discrepancy in a pull-request review; absence of a discrepancy is not accepted until the kernel build also passes.
strategy_proposals.rationale: This directly attacks informal-to-formal mismatch, which finite controls and the Lean kernel cannot fully detect.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol principal performed synthesis, source audit, artifact design, and experiment interpretation. One GPT-5.6 Terra source-discriminator delegate supplied an advisory memo whose hash was verified; every relied-on claim was rechecked against sources or deterministic artifacts. Tools used: web search/opening, GitHub and authoritative documentation, Python 3.12.3 exact enumeration, the computational-researcher experiment recorder, shell diagnostics, and Lean 4.32.0 for environment checks. No Mathlib/Formal Conjectures kernel build, CAS, SAT/SMT solver, cloud computation, repository CI, or external expert validation was available.; orchestration: gpt-5.6-sol principal with gpt-5.6-terra delegates.

approach: Authoritative McKay corpus acquisition followed by fail-closed validation and strengthening of the actual-record parser audit

strategy.mechanism: A frozen byte preflight gates two exact forbidden-set evaluators, a third NetworkX graph6 signature path, nauty complementation, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: The authoritative corpus is retrievable unchanged and can be audited through a third independent parser without weakening the source preflight.
discriminating_test: Attempt one bounded authoritative download; admit no graph unless the 47,888-byte SHA-256 discriminator matches; regression-test the new parser path on every order-7 isomorphism class and complement.
summary: The authoritative download failed DNS resolution with curl code 6 and left no corpus. The target preflight returned code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false. The corpus gate was strengthened to require NetworkX signatures on every admitted record and complement. That path passed on all 1,044 unlabeled order-7 classes and their complements, with exact checker agreement and byte-exact nauty complementation. No order-42 graph was checked and no Ramsey bound changed.
rationale: The positive result is verification infrastructure only. The exhaustive small control tests the new independent parsing obligation, while absent authenticated bytes preclude every target-instance, novelty, or repair claim.
claims: The corpus gate now requires a third NetworkX order, edge-count, and ordered-degree signature for every source record and complement.
The new path agreed with both exact checker parsers on 2,088 order-7 source/complement records.
Local graph6 complementation agreed byte-for-byte with nauty-complg on that suite.
Zero order-42 corpus records were checked.
The rigorous range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
evidence: .proof-experiments/20260720-200524-16f6f3: acquisition returned code 6 and left no source file
next_steps: Acquire unchanged r55_42some.g6 through a new network path or user-provided authoritative file with provenance.
Require 47,888 bytes, 328 LF-terminated records, and SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb.
Run the enhanced authenticated-corpus command recorded verbatim in CHECKPOINT.md.
Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 only after the corpus passes.
Do not start unknown-42 or K43 repair search before both raw-source gates pass.

independent_checker: Checker A uses Python, a separate graph6 parser, and literal 5-subset enumeration. Checker B uses C, another parser, and recursive bitset clique enumeration. NetworkX 3.3 supplies a third decoding/signature path, and nauty-complg checks complement bytes. None ran on order 42 because the source was absent.
transfer_insights: Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> an external parser should expose shared graph6 errors -> NetworkX agreed on every order-7 source/complement control.
Fail-closed provenance -> missing bytes must prevent mathematical evaluation -> the strengthened gate recorded downstream_checks_run=false.

approach: Mandatory source/status audit, two-encoding bounded harness validation, adversarial audit of the 13 displayed OEIS A076445 terms, and construction of a fail-closed witness checker.

strategy.mechanism: Separate membership and factorization checks from exhaustive completeness; reconstruct finite claims using materially different exact encodings and reject the OEIS interval inference without a cover artifact.

hypothesis: The canonical a^2*b^3 generator and an independent prime-exponent classifier agree on every powerful integer through 10^6, while exact factor audits validate the 13 displayed A076445 terms but cannot certify that the displayed prefix is complete.
discriminating_test: Require exact list equality on [1,10^6], known controls, and exact prime-factor checks of all displayed OEIS endpoints and middles; separately inspect the source trail for a completeness artifact.
summary: The problem remains open. Two exact encodings agreed on 2,027 powerful integers through 10^6, with distance-two starts 25 and 70225 and no triple. Exact factorizations validate all 13 displayed OEIS pairs and reject every displayed middle. No search code, exhaustive domain, or completeness certificate was found, so the reported 7.38e28 exclusion was not independently reproduced.
rationale: The mandatory baseline is complete, the verification contract is now fail-closed, reusable bounded and witness checkers exist, and the evidentiary gap behind the headline computational bound has been isolated without inflating the bounded results.
claims: A hypothetical triple start is congruent to 7, 27, or 35 modulo 36; this is the known Beckon restriction.
Exactly 2,027 powerful integers occur in [1,10^6]; independent exact encodings agree, and no powerful triple occurs there.
For each of the 13 displayed A076445 values n, both n and n+2 are powerful and n+1 is not.
The inspected public artifacts do not independently certify that those 13 values exhaust all distance-two powerful pairs below the largest displayed value.
evidence: .proof-experiments/20260720-203822-2f8c58: independent SPF exponent reconstruction exactly matched all 2,027 terms and controls.
.proof-experiments/20260720-204059-94e77d: all 39 displayed pair/middle factorizations checked; output explicitly records completeness_certificate=false.
python3 -m unittest -v tests/test_verify_powerful_triple.py: four adversarial tests passed.
next_steps: Recover the T. D. Noe and Geoffrey Reynolds algorithm and exact domain behind the A076445 prefix.
Implement a toy Sentance/Mollin-Walsh Pell-kernel cover and prove it enumerates every distance-two powerful pair through 10^6.
Require exact agreement with artifacts/baseline_powerful_1e6.json before scaling.
If a complete historical artifact is found, replay it independently and design a compact completeness certificate rather than extending the cutoff.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-203815-fbb4dd: generated 2,027 powerful integers through 10^6; pair starts [25,70225], no triples.
.proof-experiments/20260720-203822-2f8c58: independent factor-exponent classifier matched exactly.
.proof-experiments/20260720-204059-94e77d: displayed OEIS factorizations verified, completeness not certified.
independent_checker: scripts/check_powerful_enumeration.py reconstructs the complete bounded set using prime exponents rather than a^2*b^3 generation. scripts/check_oeis_first13.py independently rechecks factor output using exact products and deterministic primality below 2^64. Any positive witness still requires an external Sage, PARI, or certificate-based reconstruction.
transfer_insights: Pell classification of consecutive odd powerful pairs -> an exhaustive kernel/fundamental-unit cover should reproduce every distance-two pair in a toy interval -> source parametrization recovery remains the next test.
Certificate-carrying counterexample search -> three complete factorizations can validate a finite witness independently of its search process -> fail-closed local checker completed.
Formal premise retrieval -> the mod-36 restriction should be a compact Lean normalization lemma -> proposed but not executed during this baseline.

established_facts.claim: Any hypothetical triple start n is congruent to 7, 27, or 35 modulo 36.
established_facts.evidence: Elementary mod-4/mod-9 proof; published by Beckon in 2019.

established_facts.claim: The two independent implementations find 2,027 powerful numbers, pair starts 25 and 70225, and no triple through 10^6.
All 13 displayed A076445 values are valid distance-two powerful-pair starts and each displayed middle is non-powerful.
The abc conjecture implies only finitely many powerful triples.
established_facts.evidence: Nitaj 1996, Proposition 3.8.3, and the official source summary.

ruled_out.reason: Membership and factorization checks do not exclude omitted distance-two pairs.
ruled_out.evidence: .proof-experiments/20260720-204059-94e77d records completeness_certificate=false; OEIS history supplies attribution but no exhaustive artifact.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Obtain a complete search program with a proven domain or an independently checkable exhaustive certificate.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Naively enumerate a^2*b^3 values to 7.38e28.
ruled_out.reason: The b=1 branch alone exceeds 2.7e14 iterations and would merely replay an arbitrary cutoff.
ruled_out.evidence: scripts/enumerate_powerful.py loop bounds and the integer square root of the reported bound.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A proven sublinear pair enumerator or kernel bound making certified exhaustive coverage tractable.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Transfer van Doorn's growing-difference progressions directly to consecutive integers.
ruled_out.reason: The construction has common difference 2*sqrt(N)+1 and addresses a different adjacency question.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A powerfulness-preserving transformation that provably reduces the difference to 1.

Beckon proves the mod-36 condition; this epoch uses it as a known control rather than claiming novelty.

approach: Fail-closed order-42 corpus acquisition through a new mirror path, plus complete-adjacency parser hardening

strategy.mechanism: A frozen byte preflight gates two exact evaluators, full upper-triangle agreement across two custom parsers and NetworkX, nauty complementation, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: The Hugging Face resolve path can supply the frozen corpus bytes, and full adjacency fingerprints can eliminate the remaining parser-equivalence weakness.
discriminating_test: Attempt one bounded mirror download; require 47,888 bytes, 328 LF records, and the frozen SHA-256 before target work; require all three parsers to agree on complete adjacency.
summary: The new Hugging Face acquisition path failed DNS resolution with curl code 6 and left no corpus or HTTP headers. The final preflight returned code 2 with downstream_checks_run=false. The verifier was strengthened from order/edge/degree signatures to complete upper-triangle fingerprints. Its exhaustive order-7 regression passed on all 1,044 unlabeled classes and their complements, including exact evaluator agreement, semantic mutations, malformed-input rejection, and byte-exact nauty complementation. Zero order-42 records were checked and no Ramsey bound changed.
rationale: The source blocker remains, but complete fingerprints remove a concrete verification gap that coarse graph signatures could miss. The result is scoped research-software progress, not target-order mathematical evidence.
claims: Python, C, and NetworkX produced identical complete adjacency fingerprints for 2,088 order-7 source/complement records.
The two exact evaluators agreed, and local complement bytes matched nauty-complg throughout that suite.
The provisional K43 controls still pass, but publisher authentication remains false.
No order-42 graph was evaluated; the rigorous range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
next_steps: Do not retry ANU or Hugging Face curl through the unchanged command network.
Obtain unchanged McKay bytes through a genuinely new export path or a user-supplied file with retrieval provenance.
Run the authenticated-corpus command recorded in CHECKPOINT.md and stop at the first mismatch.
Authenticate Springer Supplementary Data 4 only after the corpus gate passes.

approach: Second authoritative retrieval followed by one non-overlapping full fail-closed order-42 corpus gate

strategy.mechanism: Hash-linked HTTPS provenance gates two exact evaluators, three full-adjacency parser paths, target nauty complementation, structural controls, and canonical labeling.

hypothesis: A second direct authoritative retrieval reproduces the frozen McKay bytes and the supplied 328 records plus their 328 complements pass every exact control gate.
discriminating_test: Require immutable raw HTTP headers, body, transfer metadata, and experiment record, then stop on the first provenance, byte, parser, evaluator, complement, histogram, degree, or canonical-label mismatch.
summary: The authoritative HTTP/2 200 response omitted Content-Type, so provenance schema v2 now preserves that absence instead of inferring text/plain. The second 47,888-byte body matched SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb. One full gate passed on 328 source records and 328 complements in 997.687 seconds. No Ramsey bound or completeness claim changed.
rationale: Two materially different exact checkers found no forbidden 5-set; NetworkX agreed on every 861-bit adjacency fingerprint; nauty-complg matched byte-for-byte; the expected histogram and degree range matched; and nauty-labelg produced 656 distinct supplied classes.
claims: The authoritative corpus was retrieved twice with identical 47,888-byte bodies and SHA-256 067902e853d87b49bcef0d1d4c0e3bbadd238ee18bc65341b079a3ca4780eccb.
The captured authoritative response omitted Content-Type; the accepted provenance makes no inferred media-type claim.
Exactly the supplied 328 order-42 records and their 328 complements pass the complete local control gate.
This does not prove corpus completeness and does not change 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46.
next_steps: Authenticate unchanged Springer Supplementary Data 4 using the exact retrieval command in CHECKPOINT.md.
Dual-check its conflict identities and only then canonicalize valid deletions against the supplied 656.
After source authentication, execute one bounded six-vertex conflict-core repair slice.

independent_checker: Checker A uses Python Boolean matrices and direct 5-subset enumeration. Checker B separately parses graph6 in C and uses recursive bitset clique search. NetworkX supplies a third parser, while nauty-complg and nauty-labelg supply external complement and canonical-label implementations.
transfer_insights: Fail-closed data provenance -> a missing response header must remain missing -> schema v2 preserved Content-Type as null and rejected authority, byte-count, inference, and header-hash mutations.
Isolated adversarial reconstruction -> correlated local graph transformations need an external representation -> nauty and NetworkX agreed on all target records.

closest_prior_work.difference: McKay supplies the historical graphs; this artifact adds immutable transport provenance and multiple exact verification paths without claiming a new graph or classification.

novelty_searches.source: Dynamic Survey DS1.18 and McKay maintained data page
novelty_searches.query: R(5,5) current bounds and order-42 graph corpus
novelty_searches.finding: The maintained range remains 43 <= R(5,5) <= 46, and the 656 count remains strong evidence rather than a proved complete census.

external_channel.acceptance_path: Obtain a clean third-party replay before considering maintainer contact; do not present this artifact as a Ramsey-number result.

continuation.objective: Authenticate the exact publisher K43 two-conflict seed.
continuation.first_action: Run the Springer raw-TXT retrieval command recorded at CHECKPOINT.md lines 120-135.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop on transport ambiguity, byte instability, parse ambiguity, or checker disagreement; do not begin repair before the source gate passes.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol was principal investigator. Two precomputed GPT-5.6 Terra memos supplied advisory reconnaissance; Sol hash-verified them and independently implemented, mutation-tested, and executed the decisive gate. No new subagent was spawned. Deterministic tools were Python 3.12.3, C/GCC, curl 8.5.0 with OpenSSL 3.0.13, NetworkX 3.3, nauty, SHA-256, Git diagnostics, and the computational-researcher experiment harness. Web browsing checked the maintained survey and authoritative data pages. No SAT solver or proof assistant was used.; orchestration: gpt-5.6-sol principal with gpt-5.6-terra delegates.

approach: Mandatory source/status baseline followed by a bounded counterexample search that enumerates 3-full m=n+1, with a separate dense smallest-prime-factor oracle and reverse-orientation controls.

strategy.mechanism: Generate every 3-full m in the bounded domain exactly once from increasing prime supports and exponents at least three, factor m-1 exactly, and independently compare the generated set with a dense sieve oracle.

hypothesis: The sparse generator is complete and duplicate-free on a nontrivial audited overlap, and either finds a target witness with n+1<=100000000 or certifies an empty hit list only on that bounded domain.
discriminating_test: Compare the complete generated 3-full set against a dense smallest-prime-factor scan through 10^6, exercise reversed examples, then enumerate all 3-full m through 10^8.
summary: The official and original statements agree on the target orientation: n is 2-full and n+1 is 3-full. The maintained source remains open. A dense oracle and sparse generator agreed on exactly 307 3-full values through 10^6, with identical canonical SHA-256 hashes and no duplicates. The sparse search then examined 1,645 3-full values through 10^8 and found no target. No universal claim or candidate solution is made.
rationale: The source audit resolves the major orientation hazard, while the independent dense encoding validates the sparse generator on a meaningful overlap. The 10^8 computation is rigorous only within its explicit range and is retained as baseline infrastructure rather than novelty.
claims: The original 1980 question asks whether B_2(n)=n and B_3(n+1)=n+1 has no solution; n=8 belongs to the separately stated reverse question.
The sparse and dense encodings produce the same 307 3-full integers through 10^6, with canonical set SHA-256 b1be51e2d05797f9e1e1908a378dbfd0dda61478f7399354e08d9510791f5a12.
There is no target witness with n+1<=100000000; exactly 1,645 3-full values were exhaustively examined.
Assuming the abc conjecture, only finitely many target pairs exist, but no effective unconditional search bound follows.
evidence: .proof-experiments/20260720-225425-8b9ae9: dense/sparse equality through 10^6, 307 values, empty hit list
.proof-experiments/20260720-225435-3a467c: exhaustive sparse search through 10^8, 1,645 values, empty hit list
next_steps: Investigate the structural branch n+1=p^e with prime p and e>=3 by deriving the exact cyclotomic factor and gcd conditions for p^e-1.
Regress the structural branch against every single-prime-support case already covered through 10^6.
Inspect the provenance of OEIS A060355's reported table below 10^22 for executable search code or a completeness argument before attempting any larger bound.
experiments: .proof-experiments/20260720-225355-1577b9 — self-test passed through 10^5
.proof-experiments/20260720-225425-8b9ae9 — independent encodings agreed through 10^6
.proof-experiments/20260720-225435-3a467c — no hit among all 1,645 3-full m<=10^8
.proof-experiments/20260720-225442-7f669d — reverse fixture correctly rejected
independent_checker: tools/check_erdos366_discriminator.py uses a dense smallest-prime-factor sieve and scans every integer, materially differing from the recursive sparse generator. Through 10^6 the canonical sets and hashes agree exactly.
transfer_insights: Walker/Pell classification of consecutive 2-full pairs -> known powerful-pair families might contain target witnesses -> the known 8 and 12167 orientations fail because their successors are not 3-full, so Pell membership alone is insufficient.
Shiu's sparse structure of 3-full numbers -> enumerate m=n+1 rather than densely scanning n -> the sparse method examined only 1,645 candidates through 10^8 and matched a dense oracle through 10^6.

abc radical bounds -> asymmetric exponents 1/2 and 1/3 should force conditional finiteness -> the residual exponent is (1-5 epsilon)/6>0 for epsilon<1/5, but this supplies no unconditional effective bound.

established_facts.claim: The target orientation is n 2-full followed by n+1 3-full; n=8 is reverse-oriented.
The two independent encodings agree on all 307 3-full integers through 10^6 and find no target.
No target occurs with n+1<=100000000.
Only finitely many target pairs exist if abc is true.
established_facts.evidence: Official discussion's radical argument with epsilon<1/5

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Use (8,9), (12167,12168), or generic Pell-generated powerful pairs as target evidence without checking orientation and exponent three.
ruled_out.scope: Known reverse examples and unfiltered consecutive-powerful families
ruled_out.reason: They prove 3-full then 2-full, or only 2-full then 2-full.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Produce a family member whose first number independently passes 2-full and whose successor passes 3-full.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Routine extension of the same arbitrary search cutoff as a contribution.
ruled_out.scope: Larger bounds without a recognized benchmark, structural theorem, or certificate-bearing reproduction
ruled_out.reason: A finite null search cannot settle universal nonexistence and would not improve a verified best-known result.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: A predeclared recognized bound, explicit expert request, or new structural pruning theorem.

ruled_out.claim_or_route: Treat OEIS A060355 alone as a certified target exclusion through 10^22.
ruled_out.scope: The current b-file without retained exhaustive-search code
ruled_out.reason: It is a superset table and lacks an independently checked completeness certificate for the reported range.
ruled_out.reopen_condition: Recover or reproduce the enumeration with a complete generator and independent certificate.

open_leads.description: Analyze n+1=p^e using the factorization of p^e-1.
open_leads.rationale: Cyclotomic factor coprimality may yield a genuine obstruction or materially prune a witness branch.
open_leads.next_experiment: Derive gcd conditions, implement a bounded certified shell, and compare with all single-prime-support cases through 10^6.

open_leads.rationale: A certificate-bearing complete powerful-pair list could be filtered into a rigorous problem-366 exclusion.
open_leads.next_experiment: Audit OEIS history and Donovan Johnson table provenance for code and completeness arguments.

open_leads.description: Formalize a future explicit witness in Lean.
open_leads.rationale: The existing reverse example shows numeral instances of Nat.Full can be discharged by normalization.
open_leads.next_experiment: After a witness exists, instantiate the existential and compile with no sorry or admit.

blocker: No witness is known, and no unconditional theorem reduces universal nonexistence to a finite certified computation. General consecutive-powerful classifications do not enforce the asymmetric 3-full successor condition.
reopen_condition: Resume undifferentiated bound extension only with a recognized target bound, an explicit request, a completeness-certificate objective, or a new structural pruning result.

candidate_profile.scholarly_question: A reproducible baseline, orientation-safe witness checker, and independently audited bounded search infrastructure for Erdős problem 366.
candidate_profile.meaningful_delta: Adds a materially different dense oracle, canonical set hash, explicit reverse fixtures, precise verification contract, and source/literature checkpoint; it does not improve a recognized mathematical bound or solve the problem.
candidate_profile.acceptance_test: A fresh checkout reproduces the four recorded experiments and the dense and sparse canonical hashes agree through 10^6.

closest_prior_work.difference: Maintains the open question and contextual remarks but does not provide this reproducibility packet.
Classifies consecutive powerful pairs but does not enforce a 3-full successor or supply a problem-366 search certificate.
Lists consecutive powerful pairs but does not provide an independently checked exhaustive certificate for the target.

novelty_searches.finding: Open and verifiable; no claimed partial or complete solution.
Recovered the original orientation and nearby classical powerful-number literature; no target solution found.
A reusable superset table with 39 b-file terms below 10^22, but no retained exhaustive certificate was identified.

novelty_searches.query: powerful numbers abc; consecutive powerful numbers
novelty_searches.finding: Found conditional and nearby work, not an unconditional resolution of the asymmetric target.

external_channel.acceptance_path: Only after a witness or rigorous new theorem passes independent factorization, novelty review, human number-theory review, and Charlie approval, submit the evidence through the official problem page; a Lean certificate may separately be proposed to Formal Conjectures.

continuation.objective: Test the single-prime-support successor branch for structural information.
continuation.first_action: Write the exact factor/gcd decomposition of p^e-1 for n+1=p^e, then enumerate e>=3 and p^e<=10^6 as a regression fixture against tools/erdos366_discriminator.py.
continuation.stop_condition: Stop immediately on a verified witness; otherwise stop after the declared shell and retain the route only if it proves a local obstruction or material pruning rule.

strategy_proposals.mechanism: Decompose p^e-1 into cyclotomic factors, exploit their controlled gcds, and test whether the powerfulness requirement forces forbidden exponent-one prime divisors.
strategy_proposals.hypothesis: At least one exponent or residue class in the single-prime n+1 branch admits a provable local obstruction or a sharply reduced finite set.
strategy_proposals.discriminating_test: Compare a symbolic residue/gcd filter with the complete single-prime cases through 10^6 and require exact out-of-sample agreement.
strategy_proposals.rationale: A structural pruning lemma would carry more information than another arbitrary global cutoff.

strategy_proposals.mechanism: Recover or independently rebuild the A060355 enumeration and verify both its coverage and every listed factorization without using the present sparse target code.
strategy_proposals.hypothesis: The reported consecutive-powerful table below 10^22 can be converted into a reproducible exhaustive certificate.
strategy_proposals.discriminating_test: Locate executable provenance or reproduce a smaller published prefix with a complete independent generator before attempting the full range.
strategy_proposals.rationale: This could turn an unverified superset report into a rigorous bounded target exclusion.

tool_disclosure: GPT-5.6 Sol was principal investigator. A GPT-5.6 Terra delegate supplied bounded source reconnaissance, the initial sparse script, and memo; Sol audited relied-on claims and wrote the independent dense encoding. Tools used: web search/retrieval, curl, Poppler rendering/text utilities, SHA-256, Git read-only inspection, CPython 3.12.3 exact integers, and the proof-factory experiment harness. SymPy 1.12 was inspected but was not part of the decisive canonical comparison. The project-scoped Lean 4.27.0/Formal Conjectures bootstrap installed its toolchain and cache; its final dependency build was stopped with SIGINT after about 39 minutes to respect the epoch ceiling, so no completed local Lean validation is claimed. No CAS, SAT/SMT solver, or proof assistant established a new mathematical result.; orchestration: gpt-5.6-sol principal with gpt-5.6-terra delegates.
